content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

June 25, 1990

Happy Chastain  
Michael Burns  
Office of Senator Charles Calderon  
State Capitol  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice  
Our File No. A-90-387

Dear Ms. Chastain and Mr. Burns:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to you concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act ("the Act").1

Your letter correctly summarizes my advice. However, since receiving your letter I have spoken with Mr. Burns and informed him that the advice is incorrect. The members of Senator Calderon's staff who are leaving the Assembly must file with the Fair Political Practices Commission Leaving Office statements of economic interests (Form 730) within 30 days of leaving the Assembly. The staff members who are joining him in the Senate must file with the Clerk of the Senate Assuming Office statements within 30 days of assuming office. This is necessary because the Assembly and Senate have separate conflict of interest codes and different filing officers.

Please accept our apologies for any inconvenience that may have been caused.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Wayne Imberi
Political Reform Consultant
